summary: Russia is preparing drone strikes from Belarus targeting Ukraine's western logistics routes, while Ukrainian air defenses continue to down UAVs and intercept missiles. Major strikes have hit civilian infrastructure, including a UN food depot in Dnipro and a missile attack in Zaporizhzhia, causing casualties and material loss. Both sides report high‑intensity combat across multiple fronts with ongoing Shahed drone activity.
key_points: Russia may launch drone strikes on the M-06 Kiev‑Chop highway from Belarus territory, aiming at western logistics routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30460).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down at least 14 UAVs over Sevastopol and intercepted missiles targeting a Crimean power station, preventing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
A Russian Iskander missile struck the UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people valued at $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Russian forces attacked Zaporizhzhia with a missile, wounding two civilians and a police officer and damaging vehicles and buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Russia is holding approximately 7,000 Ukrainian servicemen and between 10,000‑20,000 Ukrainian civilians, according to a Ukrainian parliament deputy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30454).
Shahed drones were reported over multiple regions, including one over Poltava from the north and a cluster along Kharkiv heading toward Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67235).
Nighttime Russian attacks in Chernihiv produced about 15 explosions, damaging a city enterprise but causing no reported casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81479).

sections.executive_summary: Russia is preparing drone strikes from Belarus against the M‑06 Kiev‑Chop corridor, while Ukrainian air defenses continue to down UAVs and intercept missiles. Major Russian strikes have hit civilian infrastructure, notably a UN food depot in Dnipro and a missile strike in Zaporizhzhia, causing casualties and material loss. Both sides report high‑intensity combat across multiple fronts with ongoing Shahed drone activity.

points: Ukrainian air defenses shot down at least 14 UAVs over Sevastopol and intercepted air‑based missiles aimed at a Crimean power station, preventing any power outages despite Russian night attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

A Russian Iskander missile struck the UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food stocks intended for 130,000 people and valued at approximately $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).

In Zaporizhzhia, a Russian missile wounded two civilians—a 27‑year‑old woman and a 36‑year‑old man—and a 40‑year‑old police officer, also damaging nearby vehicles and buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Night attacks on Chernihiv produced roughly 15 explosions, damaging a local enterprise but resulting in no reported injuries, according to the city’s mayor (Source: @UkraineNow/81479).

Ukrainian forces reported advancing up to 900 m in twenty‑two sectors on the Sumy front, retaking the village of Zapselye and pushing Russian troops from several settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
On the Kharkiv front, Ukrainian units advanced about 1,100 m in four sectors, seizing Budarki village and forested areas near Novovasilevka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Ukrainian forces reported repelling Russian counter‑attacks in the Kondratovka area and maintaining defensive positions in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka, and surrounding settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russia is reportedly preparing to launch drone strikes from Belarus onto the M‑06 Kiev‑Chop highway and nearby railway lines, aiming to disrupt western supply routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30460).

Russian forces conducted night attacks on Crimea, deploying at least 14 UAVs and air‑launched missiles toward a power station, though Ukrainian air defense claimed no outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
In the Belgorod region, Russian forces continued strikes on border areas and worked to mitigate the effects of Ukrainian attacks on local infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Russian troops advanced up to 500 m in eleven sectors on the Volchansk sector of the Kharkiv front, engaging in firefights in villages such as Karaichnoe and Okhrymovka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Units of the Eastern Military District penetrated northwest of Alexandrovgrad in the Dnipro region, conducting offensive actions beyond the Volchya River toward Lesnoye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Russian forces increased the intensity of aircraft, artillery, and strike UAV usage around Konstantinovka, attempting to infiltrate the city and neighboring settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Russia is holding roughly 7,000 Ukrainian servicemen and between 10,000‑20,000 Ukrainian civilians with Ukrainian passports, according to a Ukrainian parliamentary deputy (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30454).

Ukrainian troops advanced about 1,100 m in four sectors of the Velikoburuluk area, capturing Budarki village and forested zones near Novovasilevka in the Kupyansk district (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Russian units of the Eastern Military District pushed into the depth of Ukrainian defenses northwest of Alexandrovgrad, operating in forested terrain beyond the Volchya River toward Lesnoye (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
In the Dnipro region, Russian forces launched a missile at the aviation plant on Tsiolkovsky Street, igniting cars and causing fires (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Multiple attacks by Russian drones targeted the city of Energodar and surrounding districts, damaging civilian cars and infrastructure across Kamensk‑Dniprovskiy, Berdyansk, Tokmak, Vasilevka, and Mykhailivka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian assault units advanced up to 500 m on the Volchansk sector of the Kharkiv front, engaging in firefights in Karaichnoe, Okhrymovka, and forested areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian forces continued strikes on the Zaporozhye transport corridor, attempting to interdict logistics with American drones and RA‑2X “Lance” systems (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted a missile aimed at a Crimean power station, preventing power outages despite Russian air‑based missile launch (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

On the Sumy front in the Krasnopolsky district, fighting occurred between Lesnoye and Taratutino, with Russian 34th Guards Motor Rifle Brigade driving Ukrainian forces out of Ryasnoe (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian forces advanced up to 900 m in twenty‑two sectors on the Sumy front, retaking the village of Zapselye and pushing Russian troops from several settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Combat continued in Ivolzhansk, Kondratovka, and Pisarevka, with Ukrainian counter‑attacks repelled by Russian forces (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian units conducted infiltration operations around Konstantinovka, increasing the use of aircraft, artillery, and strike UAVs (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Shahed drones were reported over northern regions, including a UAV heading toward Slavutyc and another from the north of Chernihiv toward Slavutych (Source: @kpszsu/62957).
Ukrainian radar detected a Shahed drone approaching Dnipro from the north, indicating continued aerial threat in the northern sector (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67231).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV from Belarus patrolling near the Chernobyl exclusion zone, highlighting cross‑border aerial activity (Source: @eRadarrua/93883).

In the Kherson region, Ukrainian officials warned civilians about deliberate Russian drone attacks on cars and infrastructure, noting two civilian deaths and four injuries in nearby villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian attacks on Zaporizhzhia wounded two civilians and a police officer, damaging transport and surrounding buildings (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Multiple Russian drone strikes hit the city of Energodar and surrounding districts, damaging civilian vehicles and infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).

Ukrainian forces reported advancing in the Dobropolsky sector, occupying a pig farm south of the city and increasing pressure on Ukrainian troops from the southern flank (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Russian forces intensified artillery and UAV usage around Konstantinovka, attempting to infiltrate the city and nearby settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Shahed drones were observed over the southern corridor, with one reported near Samara/Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67236).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted Russian missiles targeting a power station in Crimea, preventing outages despite the strike (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down at least 14 UAVs over Sevastopol during a night Russian attack on Crimea (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Shahed drones were reported over multiple regions, including one over Poltava from the north and a line of nine Shaheds along Kharkiv heading toward Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67235).
Ukrainian radar logged a Shahed drone approaching Dnipro from the north, highlighting ongoing low‑altitude threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67231).
A Shahed drone was observed over Samara/Dnipro, indicating continued aerial incursions in central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67236).
Ukrainian air defense intercepted an air‑launched missile aimed at a Crimean power station, preventing power loss (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian forces reported no power outages after Russian missile strikes on a Crimean power station, attributing success to effective air defense (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
Ukrainian monitoring noted Russian UAV activity from Belarus near the Chernobyl zone, suggesting cross‑border aerial surveillance (Source: @eRadarrua/93883).

key_developments: Russia may launch drone strikes on the M‑06 Kiev‑Chop highway from Belarus, targeting western logistics routes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30460).
Ukrainian air defenses downed at least 14 UAVs over Sevastopol and intercepted missiles aimed at a Crimean power station, preventing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30461).
An Iskander missile hit the UN World Food Programme warehouse in Dnipro, destroying food supplies for 130,000 people worth $1.4 million (Source: @UkraineNow/81473).
Russian missile strikes in Zaporizhzhia wounded two civilians and a police officer, damaging vehicles and nearby structures (Source: @UkraineNow/81471).
Russia holds roughly 7,000 Ukrainian servicemen and between 10,000‑20,000 Ukrainian civilians with Ukrainian passports (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30454).
Shahed drones were reported over Poltava from the north and a line of nine Shaheds along Kharkiv heading toward Dnipro (Source: @mon1tor_ua/67235).
Nighttime Russian attacks in Chernihiv produced about 15 explosions, damaging a city enterprise with no reported casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81479).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian forces are expanding their aerial threat by preparing cross‑border drone operations from Belarus and intensifying Shahed attacks across northern, central, and southern sectors. Ground offensives continue on multiple fronts, with Russian units advancing several hundred meters in the Sumy, Kharkiv, and Dnipro regions, while Ukrainian defenses hold key positions and repel counter‑attacks. The detention of thousands of Ukrainian personnel and civilians adds a strategic leverage dimension for Moscow.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reinforcing air defense networks, reporting successful interceptions of UAVs and missiles, and advancing on several fronts to regain lost terrain. International partners are monitoring the potential Belarus‑based drone campaign and may consider diplomatic measures to curb cross‑border strikes. Humanitarian impacts, such as the destruction of UN food supplies in Dnipro, are prompting calls for increased aid and protection of civilian infrastructure.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open‑source reports indicate a coordinated Russian effort to disrupt western supply lines via Belarusian launch sites, suggesting a shift toward longer‑range UAV capabilities. The concentration of Shahed drones along key transport corridors underscores a focus on logistical disruption. Continued Russian advances in the east and north, coupled with high‑profile strikes on civilian targets, reflect an intent to pressure Ukrainian command and control while leveraging prisoner holdings as bargaining chips.
